# AI Sales Revolution Backlash: 92% of B2B Teams Are Still Failing

Everyone bought into the AI sales hype. But 92% of B2B sales teams are still missing quota targets in 2024. Welcome to Year Two of the AI Sales Hangover—where more automation equals more lost revenue.

## Promises Made, Quotas Missed

AI was supposed to save B2B sales from burnout, bloated costs, and low conversion rates. Instead? Most teams are working harder and producing less.

According to recent research on B2B sales trends, a staggering 92% of teams are still underperforming, even as over 60% of companies have adopted AI tools.

“We were promised precision,” said one VP of Sales we interviewed. “What we got was friction—and floods of useless data.” [...] And board members are asking: “Where’s the uplift?”

You gave every rep AI tools. Before that, you gave them enablement content. Before that, dashboards. Is any of this helping them sell?

## One Sales Leader’s Warning

“What broke our team wasn’t AI—it was our blind faith in it,” said a Sales Director at a failed B2B SaaS startup. “We trained bots better than we trained humans. Then came missed targets and mass churn.”

The rise of AI has revealed one ugly truth: you can’t automate your way out of crappy sales leadership.

## What Happens Next?

AI isn’t going away. But it needs a reset.

Watch for the return of human-first strategies: better coaching, smarter segmentation, real buyer empathy. [...] ## From Streamlining to Overcomplicating

Here’s the dirty secret: Most AI sales tools aren’t built for salespeople. They’re built for executives, IT, or boardroom optics. Because of that, reps spend more time training software than talking to buyers.

And when sales tech doesn’t deliver, the fallout is bigger than lost pipeline. It’s morale. Turnover. Blown forecasts. Read how to support struggling teams before it’s too late.

## The Pipeline Paradox: AI Adds Volume, Not Value

AI-generated outreach looks great on dashboards—until you realize buyers see right through the automation. Reps are now sending 4x more emails but closing fewer qualified deals.

results.title: AI in B2B customer support is a slap in the face
results.content:
- Valid frustration, but I think the issue is down to poorly set up AI support rather than anything else. I'm the founder of an AI customer service business and one of our principles has always been that it has to be easy to speak to a person if someone needs to. We do this by giving multiple ways to escalate - ask to speak to a person, click a button to speak to someone, ask about specific topics AI can't handle, if we notice frustration or repetition, all of these will auto-escalate - it's the only way we can get a true reflection on the AI's performance and make it better. (and we also summarize the conversation on escalation automatically so you don't have to repeat yourself) I've had good and bad AI support experiences myself - Clay and Twilio were great examples (not using our [...] - Go onto any App Store: Apple/Google And you will find real comments of real users complaining about this exact thing From major health care companies to big software brands They just don’t care at this point Which I do think creates a market need and gap for businesses that can fill this need and provide a service or product
- I run a B2B tech support team (later stage startup) and it boils my blood whenever I'm urged to check out one of these AI tools. They are completely incapable of providing the level of support that paying customers should expect yet they're touted as a full support replacement. For what we charge our customers, the least we can do is provide competent support.
- LLM’s are the ultimate B2B grift - an evolution even. [...] - The problem isn't even the AI part. It's that these companies are using bots as a cost cutting measure disguised as a feature. They don't want you to reach a human. The bot isn't broken, it's working exactly as intended. Deflect deflect deflect until the customer gives up or figures it out themselves. I run a couple businesses and we pay good money for B2B tools. The idea that I'm paying five figures a year for software and can't talk to a person when something breaks is insane. And you're right about the summarization thing, that's the part that really tells on them. They have the technology to make the handoff smooth and they just... don't. Because the whole point was never to help you faster. It was to make support cheaper for them.

# Smarte Review: Modern B2B Sales Intelligence Platform Built for Revenue Teams That Scale

Jason KimBy Jason KimNo Comments7 Mins Read

Sales Intelligence Platform showcasing Smarte’s B2B data.

Share

Facebook   Twitter   LinkedIn   Pinterest   Email

B2B sales has changed radically. Buyers are more educated, decision cycles are longer, and inboxes are flooded with generic outreach. In this environment, success is no longer based on how many leads a team can gather but on how intelligently the leads are identified, understood, and engaged. [...] Table of Contents

Toggle

## What Is Smarte?

Smarte is an AI-powered B2B sales intelligence and data enrichment platform designed to help sales, marketing, and revenue operations teams identify the right prospects and engage them with context.

Unlike traditional tools for generating leads that deliver static lists, Smarte delivers continuously updated contact and company intelligence. This includes verified business emails, direct dials, firmographic data, and contextual signals to know when a prospect may be ready to have a conversation.

The positioning and core capabilities of the platform are described on the official Smarte sales intelligence platform, which is introduced as a unified solution for prospecting, enrichment, and revenue intelligence and not as a data tool. ## Search HGInsights

 Published May 4, 2026

# Essential AI Sales Tools Used by Modern B2B Revenue Teams

Picture of Susan Torrey

Susan Torrey

Essential AI Sales Tools Used by Modern B2B Revenue Teams

AI sales tools are changing the way modern revenue teams plan, prioritize, and execute across the entire GTM motion. Your sales leaders and RevOps teams can’t rely on intuition and static lists anymore since buying journeys have dramatically shifted, and data volumes have exploded as a result. The right AI sales software helps your team focus on accounts that actually convert, instead of chasing activity that looks busy but never turns into revenue.

## Why AI has become core to modern B2B sales [...] ### Faster follow-up and deal momentum

AI alerts your reps when buyer behavior changes, such as surges in new research or increased engagement. Timely outreach during active buying windows increases response rates. Conversation intelligence and activity tracking help clarify next steps, and forecasting improves when signals are captured automatically rather than logged by hand.

## The role of RevOps in scaling AI sales tools

Even the most advanced AI sales software fails without operational alignment. RevOps shapes how data is governed, how scoring is defined, how workflows are built, and how your systems operate together. When RevOps leads that work, intelligence becomes something your whole organization actually uses — not a tool that lives in a separate tab. [...] ## Why AI has become core to modern B2B sales

For many revenue teams, B2B buying cycles have become extended, multi-layered, and increasingly difficult to predict accurately. Research from Salesforce indicates that only 40% of a rep’s week is spent selling, while 57% say customers take longer to decide. At the same time, 69% of buyers expect measurable ROI, and 67% expect personalization. Buyers are conducting independent research, often using AI themselves.

# Why Sales Prospecting Software for Modern B2B Teams

Modern B2B sales teams lose 21% of productivity to “Architectural Fragmentation.” Successful 2026 prospecting requires a unified platform that collapses discovery, technographic triangulation, and native CRM integration into a single workflow.

Gro Sales Prospecting Dashboard

Trusted by leading teams worldwide

## Table of Contents

## The Efficiency Gap: Legacy vs. Modern Stack

Enterprise sales teams waste 21% of their day on data entry. Not selling. Not having conversations. Building lists. The problem is in its architecture. Your sales prospecting software lives scattered across five different systems: LinkedIn for discovery, enrichment tools for cleanup, CRM for storage, and an intent platform for prioritization. [...] While LinkedIn is a powerful tool for initial contact discovery, it lacks the critical automation and prioritization layers needed for modern high-velocity sales. Gro acts as a "LinkedIn Sales Navigator alternative" by completing the workflow with automated connection requests, AI-driven follow-ups, and native CRM synchronization. Our platform allows you to ingest Sales Navigator URLs and then enriches that data with verified emails and phone numbers that LinkedIn doesn't provide. Furthermore, Gro adds a layer of buying stage intelligence, helping you understand which of your connections are actually ready for a sales conversation. By moving beyond the limitations of a single channel, you gain an omnichannel advantage that captures prospects wherever they are most active.
